data.content: Let me explore the source files in depth to understand what needs AOT-compatibility changes.

arguments.name: explore-aot
arguments.prompt:
Explore the project in /tmp/sv-4be882f2dfff4761b3cbf977c489815d/before/ to identify all AOT-compatibility issues. This is a .NET 9 C# project (Azure.ResourceManager).

For AOT compatibility, look for:
1. Reflection usage (Type.GetType, Activator.CreateInstance, Assembly.GetTypes, etc.)
2. JSON serialization using System.Text.Json without source generators (JsonSerializer.Serialize/Deserialize without JsonSerializerContext)
3. Dynamic code generation (Reflection.Emit, Expression trees compiled at runtime)
4. MakeGenericType/MakeGenericMethod calls
5. Any [DynamicallyAccessedMembers] attributes that may be needed
6. Converter patterns that use reflection

Read ALL .cs files in the project, including subdirectories (Common/, Shared/, Extensions/, Resources/, ManagementGroup/, Properties/). List every file and the specific AOT issues found (with line numbers and code snippets).

Also check the .csproj file for missing AOT-related settings like IsAotCompatible, PublishAot, etc.
